sourceMovie.title: GoodFellas
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: GoodFellas (1990) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Martin Scorsese's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The drama and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Irishman
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If GoodFellas clicked for you, The Irishman (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and crime, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If GoodFellas clicked for you, Casino (1995) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and crime, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after GoodFellas?
faq.answer: Try The Irishman, Casino, Once Upon a Time in America—each shares DNA with GoodFellas while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is GoodFellas?
faq.answer: GoodFellas (1990) is best described as Drama, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is GoodFellas worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
